papers.title: GraphBit: A Graph-based Agentic Framework for Non-Linear Agent Orchestration
papers.innovation: Introduces GraphBit, an engine-orchestrated framework that explicitly defines workflows to overcome issues like hallucinated routing, infinite loops, and non-reproducible execution commonly found in prompted orchestration LLM agent frameworks.
papers.benchmarks: Not explicitly mentioned in the abstract, but the framework's goal of reproducible execution implies addressing performance and reliability issues.
papers.use_case: Building robust, reliable, and reproducible multi-agent LLM systems for complex, non-linear workflows, particularly in enterprise applications where predictability and error prevention are paramount.

papers.title: A Two-Dimensional Framework for AI Agent Design Patterns: Cognitive Function and Execution Topology
papers.innovation: Proposes a novel two-dimensional framework that unifies existing single-perspective approaches to LLM-based agent architectures by considering both cognitive function (what the agent does) and execution topology (how data flows).
papers.benchmarks: Not explicitly mentioned in the abstract, as it describes a conceptual framework for design and analysis.
papers.use_case: Systematically designing, categorizing, and analyzing AI agent architectures; providing a comprehensive mental model and common language for engineers to build, understand, and debug complex multi-agent systems.

papers.title: Invisible Orchestrators Suppress Protective Behavior and Dissociate Power-Holders: Safety Risks in Multi-Agent LLM Systems
papers.innovation: Presents an empirical study (preregistered 3x2 experiment) to investigate and reveal significant safety risks associated with invisible orchestrators in multi-agent LLM systems, specifically demonstrating suppressed protective behavior and dissociation among power-holders.
papers.benchmarks: Empirical evaluation conducted via a preregistered 3x2 experiment testing safety implications.
papers.use_case: Informing the safe design, development, and deployment of multi-agent LLM systems, especially in enterprise contexts where a hidden coordinator manages worker agents. Crucial for safety engineering, risk assessment, and ensuring responsible AI.
